raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 1432 creates transparency, accountability, and a viable pathway for health facilities to achieve seismic compliance.",
    "The Alfred E Alquist Hospital Seismic Safety Act requires that by January 1, 2030, all hospital buildings have the capacity to provide patient services following an earthquake.",
    "Hospitals that do not comply with the 2030 seismic standard will be required to close their doors to patient care.",
    "The estimated cost of upgrades and construction for all California hospitals to comply with the 2030 seismic standards ranges from $34 billion to $140 billion depending on the compliance approach.",
    "The cost of upgrades would put 40% of California hospitals in 'severe financial distress' in immediate risk of bankruptcy.",
    "Community and public hospitals would take the most significant hit, further hindering access to care for many Medi-Cal and Medicare patients."
  ]
}
```
